name: Norway
summary: The country of which the narrator notes only that a certain seaman who saw things is dead — while Legrasse and his men have been let alone.
description: Norway appears in the narrator's dark accounting of what happens to those who learn too much: Legrasse and his men, it is true, have been let alone — but in Norway a certain seaman who saw things is dead.

sources.quote: Legrasse and his men, it is true, have been let alone; but in Norway a certain seaman who saw things is dead.
sources.context: Chapter 2, The Tale of Inspector Legrasse
